_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Sore Throat. Based on NICE NG84 and FeverPAIN/Centor scoring.
label: Sore Throat

questions.text: How long have you had a sore throat?
Do you have difficulty breathing or opening your mouth fully?
Do you have a fever (temperature above 38°C)?
Have you noticed a skin rash?
Do you have swollen glands in your neck?
Have you coughed in the last 24 hours?
questions.clinicalNote: Absence of cough increases likelihood of bacterial cause (Centor criterion)

questions.text: Can you see any white patches or pus on your tonsils?

redFlags.description: Difficulty breathing or trismus — possible peritonsillar abscess or epiglottitis
redFlags.message: Difficulty breathing with a sore throat can be very serious. Call 999 or go to A&E immediately.

redFlags.condition: q2 === true && q4 === true && q1 === 'More than 7 days'
redFlags.description: Severe sore throat, difficulty swallowing, high fever lasting over 7 days — possible quinsy
redFlags.message: Your symptoms suggest a possible serious throat infection. Please attend urgent care or A&E today.

redFlags.description: Sore throat with fever and rash — possible scarlet fever
redFlags.message: A rash with fever and sore throat needs GP assessment today — possible scarlet fever.

eligibilityRules.reason: Rash present — not suitable for pharmacy treatment without GP assessment

feverPainScore.description: FeverPAIN scoring to estimate likelihood of streptococcal infection

criteria.criterion: Attend rapidly — within 3 days of onset (q1 = less than 3 days)

outcomeRules.reason: High FeverPAIN score — likely bacterial infection, antibiotic prescription likely needed
Moderate FeverPAIN score — pharmacy assessment appropriate
Low FeverPAIN score — likely viral, self-care recommended

selfCareAdvice: Rest and drink plenty of fluids. Paracetamol or ibuprofen for pain relief. Gargle with warm salt water. Throat lozenges may help.
pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Throat lozenges / sprays for pain relief
Phenoxymethylpenicillin (if bacterial cause confirmed by pharmacist)
safetyNetAdvice: Seek urgent help if: you have difficulty breathing, cannot swallow fluids, or symptoms rapidly worsen.
